folio.label: 164r
folio.l: that had it biseged , and wolde it al destroye

¢ Abigail delyuäede
Nabal hire housbonde fro Dauyd the kyngö , that wolde han
slayn hi~ , & apaised the Ire of the kyngö , by hire wyt , and by
hire good cou~seillyngö 132Cross in dry point in the left margin.ODR

¢ Hester by hire cou~seil , enhaunced gretly
the peeple of god , in the regne , of Assureus the kyng ,

And æe same
bountee in good cou~seillyng , of many a good wo~man , moun men
telle

¢ And more ouä , whan that oure lord , had creat Adam oure
forme fader , he seide in this wyse ,

It is nat good , to be a man
allone , make we to hi~ an helpe , semblable to hi~ self ,

heere mou~
©e se , that if that wo~men weren nat goode , & here cou~seil good &
¸fitable ,

oure lord god of heuene , wolde neither han wrougút
he~ , ne called he~ helpe of man , but rathere co~fusiou~ of man ,

&& what is bettre than
wysdom ; wo~man , And what is bettre than good wo~man ; no æi~g ,

And sire , by manye of othere resons , moun ©e sen , æ many wo~-
men ben goode , & here cou~seil good & ¸fitable

¢ And æäfore Sire
If ©e wole troste to my cou~seil , I shal restore ©ow ©oure dougúter
hool & sou~d ,

& I wol don to ©ow so muche , that ©e shuln haue
honoåe in this cause

¢ whan Melibe had herd the wordes of
his wyf Pèdence , he seide thus ,

I se wel , that the word of Sa-
lamon is sotú , he seitú æ wordes æ ben spoken discretly by
ordynau~ce , ben honycombes , for they ©euen swetnesse to the
soule , & holsumnesse to the body ,

And wyf , by cause of thyne
swete wordes , & eke for I haue assaied , & preeued thy grete sa-
pience , & thy gret trouthe , I wol gouäne me by thy counseil , in
alle thyngö

¢ Now sire q¶ Dame Prudence , & syn ©e vouche
saf , to be gouäned by my cou~seil , I wol enforme ©ow , how ©e shuln
gouäne ©oure self , in chesyngö of ©oure cou~seillours ,

©e shuln~
first in alle ©ourö werkes , mekely byseken , to the heigú god , that
he wol be ©oure cou~seillour ,

And shapetú ©ow to swicú entente
that he ©eue ©ow cou~seil & comfort , as taugúte Tobye his sone

At alle tymes , æè shalt blisse god , & preye hi~ to dresse thyne
weyes , & loke æ alle thyne cou~seils , ben in hi~ for euäe moore ,,

¢ Seynt Iame eke seitú , If any of ©ow haue nede of Sapience
axe it of god ,

and afterward , tha~ne shuln ©e take cou~seil , in ©ourö
self , and examyne wel ©ourö thougútes , of swicú thynges , as
©ow thynketú , that is best for ©ourö ¸fyt ,

And tha~ne shuln ©e
dryue fro ©oure herte , thre thynges , æ ben contrious to good
counseil ,
that is to seyn ; Ire , Coueitise , & hastynesse

¢ First
he that axetú cou~seil of hi~ self , certes he muste ben w outen
Ire , for manye causes ,

the firste is this , he æ hatú gret Ire , and
wrathe in hi~ self , he wenetú alwey , æ he may do thyng , æ he
